qa_pairs.question: What modules are required to run GROMACS version 2020.4 on GPUs using CUDA 11.4 in the StdEnv/2020 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: To run GROMACS 2020.4 on GPUs with CUDA 11.4 in the StdEnv/2020 environment, you need to load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 cuda/11.4 openmpi/4.0.3 gromacs/2020.4`. A warning sign is associated with this configuration.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary configuration for GROMACS 2020.4 when run with CUDA 11.4 in the StdEnv/2020 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: GROMACS 2020.4, when run with CUDA 11.4 in the StdEnv/2020 environment, is configured with GCC, FlexiBLAS & FFTW.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are needed to run GROMACS version 2020.4 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2020 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: To run GROMACS 2020.4 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2020 environment, you need to load `StdEnv/2020 gcc/9.3.0 openmpi/4.0.3 gromacs/2020.4`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary configuration for GROMACS 2020.4 when run on CPUs in the StdEnv/2020 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: GROMACS 2020.4, when run on CPUs in the StdEnv/2020 environment, is configured with GCC & MKL. A warning sign is also present for this configuration.

qa_pairs.question: Which CUDA version is used for GROMACS 2020.4 when configured with GCC & MKL in the StdEnv/2020 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: For GROMACS 2020.4 configured with GCC & MKL in the StdEnv/2020 environment, it uses CUDA 11.0.

qa_pairs.question: Is the StdEnv/2018.3 software environment still supported?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the StdEnv/2018.3 software environment is deprecated and no longer supported.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are used for running GROMACS 2020.2 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: To run GROMACS 2020.2 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment, the required modules are: `StdEnv/2018.3 gcc/7.3.0 openmpi/3.1.2 gromacs/2020.2`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the notes for GROMACS 2020.2 in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment, specifically for GPU usage?
qa_pairs.answer: GROMACS 2020.2 in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment, when used with GPUs (CUDA 10.0.130), is configured with GCC & MKL and has a warning sign associated with it.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are necessary to run GROMACS 2019.6 on GPUs with CUDA in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: For GROMACS 2019.6 on GPUs in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment, you need these modules: `StdEnv/2018.3 gcc/7.3.0 cuda/10.0.130 openmpi/3.1.2 gromacs/2019.6`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the configuration for GROMACS 2019.3 running on CPUs in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: GROMACS 2019.3 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment uses the modules: `StdEnv/2018.3 gcc/7.3.0 openmpi/3.1.2 gromacs/2019.3`. The notes indicate GCC & MKL, and a dagger symbol (‡) is present, meaning double precision is not available for AVX512.

qa_pairs.question: Which CUDA version is specified for GROMACS 2018.7 GPU execution within the StdEnv/2018.3 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: GROMACS 2018.7 for GPU execution in the StdEnv/2018.3 environment uses CUDA 10.0.130.

qa_pairs.question: Is the StdEnv/2016.4 software environment still supported?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the StdEnv/2016.4 software environment is deprecated and no longer supported.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are required to run GROMACS 2018.3 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: To run GROMACS 2018.3 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment, you need the modules: `StdEnv/2016.4 gcc/6.4.0 openmpi/2.1.1 gromacs/2018.3`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the note for GROMACS 2018.3 when running on GPUs in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: GROMACS 2018.3, when run on GPUs with CUDA 9.0.176 in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment, is configured with GCC & FFTW.

qa_pairs.question: What CUDA version is specified for GROMACS 2018.2 GPU execution in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: For GROMACS 2018.2 on GPUs in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment, the specified CUDA version is 9.0.176.

qa_pairs.question: What modules are required for GROMACS 2018.1 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: The modules for running GROMACS 2018.1 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment are: `StdEnv/2016.4 gcc/6.4.0 openmpi/2.1.1 gromacs/2018.1`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the notes for GROMACS 2018.1 when running on GPUs in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: When GROMACS 2018.1 is run on GPUs with CUDA 9.0.176 in the StdEnv/2016.4 environment, the notes indicate it uses GCC & FFTW.
